name: Stuffed Artichokes in Olive Oil
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix the juice of 2 lemons and 2 tablespoons flour. Add 2 quarts water and stir thoroughly.
2. Wash artichokes; remove outer leaves, leaving only tender inner leaves. Slice off these leaves just above the heart and scoop out the fuzzy choke and remaining pinkish leaves. Cut off the stem and peel around the base with a vegetable peeler until you reach the whitish tender part.
3. As you trim each artichoke, take a lemon half, sprinkle it with salt, and rub it all around and inside the base to prevent discoloration. Then drop each artichoke into the lemon water mixture.
4. Remove jackets of fava beans. Sprinkle beans with salt and let stand for 5 minutes. Remove skins, rinse, and reserve.
5. Heat 1 cup olive oil in a skillet. Add onions and half teaspoon salt; sauté for 5 minutes. Stir in fava beans and rice. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring, then add ½ cup water. Cover and cook until the water is absorbed (about 5 minutes).
6. Stir in dill, salt, and remove from heat. Let stand covered for 15 minutes.
7. Remove artichokes from the water bath. Reserve 4½ cups liquid. Stuff the center cavity of each artichoke with rice.
8. Place each artichoke on a sheet of wax paper; sprinkle with a little water and wrap as you would a package. Arrange stem sides down in a heavy shallow pan. Add 1½ cups reserved liquid, ¼ cup olive oil, juice of half lemon, and 2 teaspoons sugar. Cover and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.
9. Reduce heat to low and simmer until artichokes are tender (about 1 hour). The liquid will be continually absorbed; add more as needed.
10. When artichokes are done, let them cool. Unwrap and arrange on a serving platter. Serve cold with garnish as part of a buffet.
